comments: Pair in the fields south of the car parks. My first time really seeing a male of this species.
Flyover, calling. Lifer. My first thought when seeing the dark hooded appearance was a Chroicocephalus gull, but noted the bulky shape, long bill, and call.
counted twelve, then saw/heard a large number in a tree.
Crow with grey neck and shoulders
At least one juv - plump songbird larger than nearby goldfinch with reddish face darker above the eye, single orangish wingbar on a dark wing, white rump, dark tail. Silent.
Bobbing up and down. Have video
Heard with Merlin (2 separate times) at same as seeing 3 sparrow sized birds.
Observed hovering repeatedly over several areas searching for prey. Later descended to the ground and was subsequently seen flying with prey held in its talons.
Observed one individual among Eiders near shore. Later observed a flock farther offshore. Identification confirmed by the distinctive yellow bill with black margins.

comments:
Grey body, darker head, red flush under tail with white flash on top.

Newbuild estate. Hopping from tree in garden to lawn over 40 minute period.
Regular at this site throughout the year
Singing from wheat beside the Ware Road, across from the midden. Distinct three note call “whit-whit-whit”.

Bird flew rapidly north along line of road at c. 20m elevation, a slender dark falcon with pointed wing tips, naked eye view from c. 100m but unmistakable; drove up track to ridge beyond cottages and scanned north - a min of 6 Swift, 6 House Martin and 5 Swallows up over North Berwick, likely double that in careful scans, but no further sign, not surprising given speed it was travelling; possibly same bird as per previous reports in June from nearby east and north coast
Seen flying over the town before presumably landing in a reedbed.
Female. Feeding with Pochard in the East corner of the Quarry. Photos and videos taken.
Single adult bird flying east over the bridge/causeway about 70m up at about 19.00; flew out of view heading towards Threipmuir Reservoir.
Female at East end with Tufted Duck
Known female associated with the Common Pochard. Larger grey coloured duck with pale cheeks and dark cap.
